name: Amoud Foundation
mission: Founded in 1998, the Amoud Foundation impacts the local and global communities by helping people help themselves through sustainable humanitarian projects while maintaining trusting relationships. Amoud is an international humanitarian organization that today serves the people in the Horn of Africa. Amoud also does work on the home-front including helping with the 2006 Hurricane Relief and 2015 Garland Tornado recovery.
theoryOfChange: Helping people help themselves through sustainable humanitarian projects and infrastructure (hospitals, wells, schools) to move beyond temporary relief to long-term dignity.
scoreSummary: AMOUD FOUNDATION shows exceptional impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Partnership with Baylor, Scott, and White Health for medical supply vetting

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.amoudfoundation.org/zakat (Source: https://www.amoudfoundation.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

all_citations.claim: The foundation holds a Charity Navigator score of 88.5/100 and operates 15 orphan centers and schools for the deaf/blind.
The charity is zakat-eligible and follows specific zakat policies and asnaf categories.
The foundation offers multiple donation options for its various humanitarian programs.
The organization reported total revenue of $11,142,566 and a program expense ratio of 89.3%.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a strong overall profile due to its high financial efficiency and direct service model, though its total score is moderated by medium data confidence.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance data transparency to improve the medium data confidence rating
Expand public reporting on specific long-term impact metrics for educational programs

dimension_explanations.alignment: There is high alignment for Muslim donors as the charity is explicitly zakat-eligible and provides clear pathways for religious giving [2]. The cause urgency is high, focusing on critical needs such as food packages and specialized education for deaf and blind students [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, maintaining a program expense ratio of 89.3% and a Charity Navigator score of 88.5/100 [1]. With a total revenue of $11,142,566 and 12.7 months of working capital, the organization shows strong financial health and the ability to sustain its 15 orphan centers and healthcare programs [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Amoud Foundation provides critical humanitarian relief and orphan care through a highly efficient financial model that directs 89.3% of expenses toward its core programs.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 12.7 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Amoud Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting vulnerable populations through healthcare, food security, and the 'Lift Up A Child' initiative, which assists 15 orphan centers and two schools for the deaf and blind [1][5]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various asnaf categories [2]. In the most recent fiscal year, the foundation reported a total revenue of $11,142,566 [5].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a strong overall profile due to its high financial efficiency and direct service model, though its total score is moderated by medium data confidence.

areas_for_improvement.context: While impact is high, the organization could benefit from <cite id="14">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway to better communicate long-term outcomes.
The foundation currently has a <cite id="15">medium data confidence rating</cite> due to limited public transparency documentation beyond mandatory filings.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization maintains high accountability standards consistent with major charity evaluators.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's 25-year track record and high financial scores mitigate concerns regarding operational stability.
risk_factors: <cite id="14">Developing status of formal theory of change</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="15">medium data confidence</cite> regarding transparency and the lack of a fully <cite id="14">documented theory of change</cite>.

data_gaps: No explicit theory of change document
confidence.notes: Strong financial data but narrative impact data is limited.

known_gaps: No explicit theory of change document

alignment.explanation: The foundation is a high fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="6">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving <cite id="7">asnaf categories including the poor and needy</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further enhanced by <cite id="18">expanding services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Amoud due to its <cite id="13">founding in 1998</cite> and its <cite id="12">perfect score for Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator. The organization manages <cite id="16">total assets of $8,810,970</cite> with clear oversight.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should publish more detailed annual impact reports to supplement its <cite id="15">current transparency profile</cite>.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="9">$3.67 cost per beneficiary</cite> and a <cite id="3">89.3% program ratio</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="17">peer median of 90.3%</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="14">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> to track long-term educational and health outcomes.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Verified outcomes with high financial efficiency

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct service delivery rather than grantmaking

rich_narrative.headline: Amoud Foundation provides critical humanitarian relief and orphan care through a highly efficient financial model that directs 89.3% of expenses toward its core programs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their zakat to directly fund orphan care and emergency relief in the Horn of Africa.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure nearly 100% of their donation reaches the field
Deeply care about orphan welfare and disability services in Somalia
Value long-standing organizations with deep roots in the regions they serve
Seek to fulfill zakat through a direct-service delivery model
giving_considerations: The organization is highly efficient but provides less public 'glossy' reporting than larger peers
Focus is heavily regional, primarily in East Africa
Financial reserves are high, indicating long-term stability but potentially less immediate 'funding gap' urgency
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-frequency multimedia impact updates or those looking for domestic US-based community programs.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has verified outcomes and an exceptional cost-per-beneficiary metric, though formal academic evaluations are not public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Amoud creates impact through direct service delivery in healthcare, food aid, and specialized education for orphans and disabled children.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high percentage of funds spent on programs actually translates into improved life outcomes for beneficiaries.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While revenue is growing, the organization holds significant reserves, suggesting it can absorb more but is not in a liquidity crisis.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Somalia, Ethiopia, Djibouti, Sudan, Togo, USA

peer_comparison.differentiator: Amoud's <cite id="3">89.3% program ratio</cite> outperforms the <cite id="17">peer median of 90.3%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The foundation directs <cite id="3">89.3% of its expenses toward core programs</cite>, ensuring minimal leakage to administrative overhead.
The organization maintains <cite id="8">12.7 months of working capital in working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer against funding fluctuations.
Amoud achieves a <cite id="9">cost per beneficiary of approximately $3.67</cite>, which is considered exceptional for international humanitarian work.

strengths_deep_dive: Amoud Foundation's financial health is anchored by its ability to scale revenue while keeping costs stagnant. With a <cite id="10">3-Year Revenue CAGR of 18.6%</cite>, the organization has demonstrated consistent growth. This growth is managed with high accountability, as evidenced by an <cite id="11">overall score of 88.88.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. Furthermore, the leadership is highly rated, earning a <cite id="12">perfect 100/100 Leadership beacon score</cite>, reflecting a stable and well-governed institutional structure that has been <cite id="13">operating for over 25 years</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1998, the Amoud Foundation has established itself as a critical provider of humanitarian relief and social services across the Horn of Africa and beyond. The organization operates with a mission to <cite id="1">provide assistance to 15 orphan centers and 2 schools for the deaf and blind</cite>, primarily serving vulnerable populations in Somalia and Somaliland. Amoud Foundation maintains a lean operational model, reporting <cite id="2">Total Revenue of $9,535,194</cite> in its most recent fiscal filing, while ensuring that a vast majority of these funds reach the field. The charity is highly efficient, maintaining a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 89.3%</cite>, which significantly exceeds the industry standard of 75%. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="4">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the organization relies on highly cost-effective methods to secure its budget. Beyond orphan care, the foundation manages diverse programs including <cite id="5">Water Is Life, Emergency Aid, Food Aid, and Health Care</cite> across countries such as Ethiopia, Djibouti, Sudan, and Togo. As a <cite id="6">zakat-eligible organization</cite>, it provides a verified pathway for Muslim donors to support the <cite id="7">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> through direct service delivery.
